source.section: 2.11 - AGM-84E SLAM Cruise Missile > 2.11.3 - Target Designation > 2.11.3.4 - Pre-Planned (PP)

steps.action: Press OSB next to MODE to toggle between TOO (Target-of-Opportunity) and PP (Pre-Planned) target designation modes. In this case, we will choose PP.
Press OSB next to MSN to enter the Mission Parameters page.
We can define up to five Pre-Planned targets per missile station: PP1 through PP5. We will designate the target on PP1. Press OSB next to another PP# to select it if you desire.
The TGT is the pre-planned target point (A/G TGT). For now, the field is empty since no target is defined yet.

(Optional) If you want to set TERM (Terminal Attack) parameters such as heading (HDG), attack angle (ANG) and velocity (VEL), press OSB next to TGTUFC, then press OSB next to TERM on the UFC. You will be able to enter terminal parameters via the UFC. However, since we will control the missile remotely during the terminal attack stage, these parameters are not required and we will leave them blank,

(Optional) We do not need O/S (Offset) parameters to the TGT since we will control the missile remotely during the terminal attack stage. Therefore, we will not need to use O/S UFC function. Parameters for Station 8

We can get target coordinates and elevation from the mission briefing or from the F10 map. We will need this information in the Deg Min Sec.Decimal-Seconds format, which can be toggled by using « LALT+Y » while being in the F10 map..
Crossed-out PP Points mean no valid coordinates are entered yet.
Make sure PP1 is selected to input Target 1 coordinates
Select
On the UFC, select ELEV (Elevation)
Select FT (« : » will appear when selected), then enter Target 1 altitude on the scratchpad (39), then press the ENT (Enter) button.
Return to main UFC menu by pressing the TGT UFC button twice.
On the UFC, select POSN (Position) « : » will appear once selected.
We will add the coordinates of Target 1 (PP1) 33 °49’17.83’’ North 35 °29’37.64’’ East
On the UFC, select LAT (Latitude). « : » will appear once selected.
Press « 2 » (N) to select North coordinates, type « 334917 », then « ENT » to enter Degrees Minutes Seconds.
Wait for the UFC screen to reset, then type « 83 », then « ENT » to enter remaining Decimal-Seconds.
On the UFC, select LON (Longitude). « : » will appear once selected.
On the UFC, press « 6 » (E) to select East coordinates, type « 352937 », then « ENT » to enter Degrees Minutes Seconds.
Wait for the UFC screen to reset, then type « 64 », then « ENT » to enter remaining Decimal-Seconds.
Return to main UFC menu by pressing the TGT UFC button twice.
Once Longitude, Latitude and Elevation coordinates are entered for PP1, the PP1 icon will be boxed and no longer crossed out.
The coordinates and elevation of the designated target point will then directly transfer to the TGT in the MSN page.
Press the OSB next to RETURN to return back to the SMS (Stores) page. We are now almost ready to begin our strike and launch the missile. Station 8 Target 1
The SLAM indication, TMR indication (time required to be within range of a valid SLAM launch) and PP indication will appear on the HUD.
Minimum and Maximum Launch Range circles will appear on the HSI (Horizontal Situation Indicator) page.
Please proceed to section
